entity.name: POOM
entity.description: POOM develops open-source edge hardware and software for real-time signal processing, embedded security workflows, NFC experimentation, and local anomaly detection. The current POOM repository provides ESP-IDF firmware and tooling for ESP32-C5-based edge hardware. We are now working on POOM XL Linux support, including complete migration of the existing POOM NFC libraries to a Linux-compatible stack and development of local edge AI anomaly detection.

projects.name: POOM Edge AI Anomaly Detection
projects.description: POOM Edge AI Anomaly Detection extends the existing ESP32-C5-based POOM open-source firmware project toward POOM XL, a Linux-based edge AI stack for real-time signal processing and anomaly detection. The project will migrate POOM's NFC libraries completely to Linux, build a local signal and network metadata pipeline, implement lightweight edge AI models for detecting abnormal patterns on-device, and generate privacy-preserving alerts and reports without centralizing raw captures. Funding will support Linux migration work, NFC library portability, real-time metadata processing, edge AI anomaly detection, documentation, tests, and reproducible public releases.

channels.description: Contact us by email to coordinate grant support, sponsorship, or other funding for the open-source POOM Edge AI Anomaly Detection project.

plans.name: Linux support and edge AI anomaly detection prototype
plans.description: One-time funding to develop POOM XL Linux support, migrate POOM's NFC libraries completely to Linux, build a real-time signal and network metadata pipeline, implement lightweight on-device anomaly detection models, create privacy-preserving alerts and reports, and publish documentation, tests, and reproducible releases.
